title: Aiko's Amazing Magnet Adventure
synopsis: When Aiko finds a mysterious magnet in her backyard, she discovers it has amazing powers that lead to hilarious mishaps around the neighborhood. With help from her diverse group of friends, she learns about magnetism while trying to control her new magnetic mayhem.

pages.text: Aiko was digging in her backyard. She loved finding cool rocks and shiny things. "Wow!" she said. "What is this?" She picked up a strange gray rock. It felt heavy and smooth.

illustration.description: A young Japanese girl with short black hair and big bright eyes kneels in a colorful garden, holding a dark gray magnet. She wears a yellow t-shirt and purple overalls. Bright flowers and green plants surround her in the sunny backyard.
illustration.alt: Aiko discovering the magnet in her garden

pages.text: Aiko put the rock in her pocket. She walked to the garage to get her bike. But something funny happened! Her dad's tools started jumping toward her pocket. "What's going on?" Aiko laughed.

illustration.description: Aiko stands in a garage with wide surprised eyes and an open mouth smile as metal tools like wrenches and screwdrivers float through the air toward her pocket. The garage has warm orange lighting with colorful tool boxes and a red bike.
illustration.alt: Tools magically flying toward Aiko in the garage

pages.text: The screwdrivers stuck to her pocket! The hammer tried to jump up too. "This rock is magic!" Aiko giggled. She didn't know she had found a super strong magnet.

illustration.description: Aiko giggles with joy as metal tools stick all over her yellow shirt and purple overalls. Her arms are spread wide in amazement. The garage background shows more tools trying to fly toward her with motion lines.
illustration.alt: Aiko covered in magnetic tools, laughing with delight

pages.text: Aiko ran outside to show her friends. She found Marcus playing with his toy cars. "Look what I found!" she said. But when she got close, all his metal cars zoomed to her pocket!

illustration.description: A young Black boy with curly hair and overalls looks amazed as his colorful toy cars fly through the air toward Aiko. The scene is on a sidewalk with a bright blue sky and green trees. Both children have big expressive eyes showing surprise and wonder.
illustration.alt: Marcus watching his toy cars fly toward Aiko

pages.text: "My cars!" Marcus laughed. "How did you do that?" Just then, Sofia rode up on her bike. Her bike bell started ringing by itself! It was trying to reach Aiko's magic rock.

illustration.description: A young Latina girl with long dark braids sits on a bright red bicycle, looking confused as her silver bike bell tilts toward Aiko. Marcus and Aiko stand nearby laughing. The neighborhood setting has colorful houses and flowering trees in the background.
illustration.alt: Sofia on her bike with the bell reaching toward Aiko

pages.text: "What's happening?" Sofia asked. Aiko pulled out the gray rock. "I think this rock has special powers!" All the metal things kept trying to stick to it. The friends were amazed!

illustration.description: All three children gather in a circle looking at the magnet in Aiko's palm with wonder-filled expressions. Various small metal objects like bottle caps and coins float in the air around them. Bright afternoon sunlight creates a magical atmosphere with warm golden tones.
illustration.alt: Three friends examining the magnet with floating metal objects

pages.text: The magnet was getting stronger! It pulled a stop sign toward Aiko. "Oh no!" she said. "I can't control it!" The friends ran down the street. Metal mailboxes leaned toward them as they passed.

illustration.description: The three children run down a suburban street with worried but excited expressions. A red stop sign tilts dramatically toward them while colorful mailboxes lean in their direction. Motion lines show the magnetic pull. The scene has a comedic, action-packed feel with bright colors.
illustration.alt: Children running as street signs and mailboxes lean toward them

pages.text: "We need help!" said Marcus. They ran to the library. Mrs. Chen, the librarian, saw them coming. "Why are all those metal things following you?" she asked with a big smile.

illustration.description: An older Asian woman with glasses and a warm smile stands in front of a colorful library. The three children approach her with various small metal objects trailing behind them like a parade. The library has bright windows and cheerful decorations.
illustration.alt: Mrs. Chen greeting the children at the library entrance

pages.text: "You found a magnet!" Mrs. Chen said. "Magnets pull metal things toward them." She showed them books about magnets. "Some magnets are very strong. That's why everything is sticking to you!"

illustration.description: Mrs. Chen sits with the three children around a low table covered with colorful books about magnets. The children listen with fascination, their eyes wide with curiosity. The library interior is warm and inviting with tall bookshelves and soft lighting.
illustration.alt: Mrs. Chen teaching the children about magnets in the library

pages.text: "Magnets have two ends called poles," Mrs. Chen explained. "They can push things away too!" She gave Aiko another magnet. When Aiko held them together, they pushed apart! "Whoa!" said Sofia.

illustration.description: Aiko holds two magnets that are pushing apart with visible force lines between them. Her expression shows amazement and concentration. Mrs. Chen, Marcus, and Sofia watch with big smiles and excited expressions. Small metal objects float gently around them in the bright library setting.
illustration.alt: Aiko demonstrating magnetic repulsion with two magnets

pages.text: Now Aiko knew how to use her magnet safely. The friends went to the park. They used the magnet to find lost metal toys in the sandbox. "This is the best treasure hunt ever!" said Marcus.

illustration.description: The three friends kneel around a sandbox at a colorful playground, using the magnet to pull up buried metal toys like small cars and spoons. Their faces show pure joy and excitement. The playground has bright equipment and green trees with blue sky above.
illustration.alt: Children using the magnet to find buried treasures in sandbox

pages.text: They helped Mr. Rodriguez pick up scattered nails from his construction work. "Thank you!" he said. "Your magnet is very helpful!" Aiko felt proud. Her magic rock wasn't just fun - it was useful too!

illustration.description: A friendly Latino construction worker in a yellow hard hat smiles gratefully as the children use the magnet to collect scattered nails. The construction site background has bright orange cones and colorful tools. Everyone looks happy and helpful in the warm afternoon light.
illustration.alt: Mr. Rodriguez thanking the children for helping collect nails

pages.text: "Science is everywhere!" Aiko said to her friends. "And it's really cool!" They spent the rest of the day exploring with their new magnetic knowledge. Every metal discovery was a new adventure!

illustration.description: All three children sit on a park bench at sunset, examining various metal objects they collected. Their faces glow with satisfaction and friendship. The magnet sits between them as the golden sunset creates a warm, magical atmosphere with purple and orange clouds.
illustration.alt: Friends sharing their magnetic discoveries at sunset

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What did Aiko find in her backyard?
questions.explanation: Aiko found a strong magnet that she thought was a magic rock because it pulled metal things toward it.

questions.question: What happened when Aiko went near Marcus's toy cars?
questions.explanation: The metal toy cars were attracted to Aiko's magnet and flew toward her pocket where she kept it.

questions.question: Who helped the children learn about magnets?
questions.explanation: Mrs. Chen, the librarian, explained how magnets work and showed them books about magnets.

questions.question: What are the two ends of a magnet called?
questions.explanation: Mrs. Chen taught them that magnets have two ends called poles, which can attract or repel other magnets.

questions.question: How did the friends use the magnet to help others?
choices: They found lost toys and collected scattered nails
questions.explanation: The friends used the magnet to find buried metal toys in the sandbox and help Mr. Rodriguez collect scattered nails from his construction work.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.instruction: Complete the story by choosing the correct words!
pages.text: Aiko found a strong _____ in her backyard. It could pull metal things toward it! The _____ stuck to her pocket. Mrs. Chen taught them that magnets have two ends called _____. The friends used the magnet to help _____ find his scattered nails.
